event.title: <show-title>Our Hero, Balthazar</show-title><event-type-title> with Live Q&A</event-type-title>
event.headline: Live Q&A with the cast and crew
event.description:
In-person Q&A with the cast and crew following the screening. On April 4th, our guests will be actors Jaeden Martell, Asa Butterfield, and Pippa Knowles; director/writer/producer Oscar Boyson; and writer Ricky Camilleri. On April 5th, Martell, Butterfield, Knowles, and Boyson will return with actor Noah Centineo and writer Ricky Camilleri, moderated by Alice Maio Mackay (director, THE SERPENT'S SKIN).

The bold comedy-drama tells the story of a wealthy New York City teenager who, eager to impress his activist crush, follows an online connection to Texas, where he believes he can stop an act of extreme violence. Anchored by a transformative performance from Asa Butterfield, the film also stars Jaeden Martell, Chris Bauer, Jennifer Ehle, Anna Baryshnikov, Noah Centineo, Becky Ann Baker, Avan Jogia, and Pippa Knowles.

eventType.title: Live Q&A
eventType.headline: Exclusive conversations with actors and filmmakers
eventType.description: Got a question for the director? Wanna meet the cast? At our special Live Q&A events, we follow the screening with an in-person discussion.

show.title: Melancholia
show.headline: A mysterious new planet threatens to collide with Earth

event.title: <show-title>Melancholia</show-title>
event.headline: A mysterious new planet threatens to collide with Earth
event.description:
On what should be the happiest day of her life, Justine (Kirsten Dunst) is grappling with severe depression. Her sister, Claire (Charlotte Gainsbourg), is trying her best to help her sister through the wedding, but her help comes across as controlling because her husband is paying for the whole extravaganza and has a rigid schedule. However, everything begins to unravel as a mysterious blue planet called Melancholia hurls towards the Earth.

Director Lars Von Trier developed MELANCHOLIA during a therapy session, when his therapist noted that depressed people tend to act calm during high-pressure events because they already anticipate the worst. Possibly his most accessible yet personal film in his oeuvre, MELANCHOLIA is a deeply beautiful examination of the human psyche under pressure.

event.title: <show-title>Twin Peaks: The Return</show-title> <event-type-title>Parts 13-15</event-type-title>
event.description:
Part 13 | 60 mins.
Cooper's return seems imminent; old secrets are unearthed. Dougie wins a jackpot, and the Mitchum brothers' storyline unfolds.

Part 14 | 60 mins.
Cooper's allies make progress; the true nature of the Black Lodge is explored. Gordon Cole has a vision, and Andy visits the White Lodge.

Part 15 | 60 mins.
Cooper's journey reaches a critical point; the past and present collide. Big Ed and Norma's romance blossoms, and Steven and Gersten's story takes a dark turn.

show.title: Daisies
show.headline: Two teenage girls embark on a series of destructive pranks

event.title: <show-title>Daisies</show-title>
event.headline: Věra Chytilová’s stunning surrealist “necrologue” of femininity in revolt
event.description:
“We’ll have to think of a worse kind of life.”

It’s difficult to feel sane and stable in our current era, with all that’s happening or not happening; it’s easy to feel as though the world has become a spoiled and unyielding place devoid of empathy and grace. This isn’t new; though the world continues to throw inventively bleak and troubling bullshit our way, filmmakers have been using the medium to break down and critique societal failures since the form's invention. In the 1960s, Czechoslovakian cinema was in the midst of a “new wave” boom, seeing the rise of new voices like Miloš Forman, Jaromil Jireš', and Věra Chytilová creating films that challenged and satirized life under the communist regime. Chytilova’s landmark film DAISIES uses surrealism and dark, absurdist humor to question the indulgences and indifferences of the privileged members of society during an era of unrest, war, and lack.

What’s the film about? Great question! Like all good art, the film asks far more questions than it answers. Two young girls, both named Marie, find themselves bored and despondent in the face of the world’s “spoiled” nature, so they decide to become just as “spoiled” as the world. The two proto-Jokers embark on a series of disruptions, pranks, and grotesque feasts while rejecting the patriarchy and seemingly losing their minds. The relationship between the Marie’s blends elements of queerness, anarchy, radical indulgence, and destruction into a pureed pulp of insanity that’s up for interpretation. Heralded as an arthouse feminist masterpiece, DAISIES was initially banned for its subversive “attacks on establishment values.” The film is now considered one of the greatest avant-garde art films ever made. And now you get to say you’ve seen it on the big screen and really be a cinephile. Not that that matters. Is that something that matters? “Do you really think it matters?”

event.title: <show-title>Twin Peaks: The Return</show-title> <event-type-title>Parts 16-18</event-type-title>
event.description:
Part 16 | 60 mins.
Cooper regains his identity; a showdown looms. Diane's true nature is revealed, and Dougie says goodbye to his family.

Part 17 | 60 mins.
Cooper confronts the ultimate evil; the truth about Laura Palmer is revealed. The final showdown with Mr. C occurs, and Judy's role is hinted at.

Part 18 | 60 mins.
Cooper's journey comes to a surreal and ambiguous conclusion. Cooper and Diane cross into another reality, leaving their fates uncertain.

show.title: The Big Lebowski
show.headline: The Coen Bros.' slacker odyssey really ties the room together

event.title: <show-title>The Big Lebowski</show-title> <event-type-title>Movie Party</event-type-title>
event.headline: It's the greatest movie party ever, but that's just like, uh, our opinion, man
event.description:
You want a movie party? We can get you a movie party, believe me. There are ways, Dude. You don't wanna know about it, believe me.

With its endlessly quotable dialogue, twist-filled mystery, and iconic characters, THE BIG LEBOWSKI has become a fixture of modern pop culture. That’s why we’re putting together a movie party to celebrate the greatness that is El Duderino (we’re not into the whole “brevity” thing).

When you arrive, we’ll provide you with a "Let's Go Bowling" bumper sticker to commemorate the forever-two-steps-behind Donny Kerabatsos, a notepad like Jackie Treehorn's, and rolling papers.

So throw on your favorite shabby sweater and roll on down for THE BIG LEBOWSKI Movie Party. It’ll be far out.

show.title: The Mummy Returns: 25th Anniversary
show.headline: Do you smell what the Scorpion King is cooking?

event.title: <show-title>The Mummy Returns: 25th Anniversary</show-title> <event-type-title>Movie Party</event-type-title>
event.headline: Do you smell what the Scorpion King is cooking?
event.description:
Director Stephen Sommers’ THE MUMMY RETURNS is not only the action-packed follow-up to his beloved 1999 original, but it also marks the big screen debut of one Dwayne Douglas “The Rock” Johnson. Nine years after condemning Imhotep (Arnold Vosloo) back to the underworld, Rick (Brendan Fraser) and Evelyn (Rachel Weisz) have settled down, had a quick-witted kid, and are living their best adventurer lives. Unfortunately for them, a reincarnated version of Anck-su-namun (Patricia Velasquez) and a few more Imhotep-worshiping knuckleheads are hellbent on bringing the mummy back so that he can claim the unstoppable power of an ancient warlord, the Scorpion King (Johnson). Cue the desert-trotting adventure.

While not as revered as its predecessor, THE MUMMY RETURNS is still a grand journey with big thrills and some laugh-out-loud moments. Fraser and Weisz bring charm in a big way, and it’s fun to see The Rock in a movie before he became one of the biggest stars in Hollywood – even if he’s covered in CGI that isn’t, like, the greatest ever. That’s all part of the charm though. Plus, Anubis’ army of jackal-headed warriors is still rad as hell.

Your ticket includes the following props:

  • Black snake – The perfect weapon to throw at your enemies.
  • Scorpion bracelet – Don't worry, you can wear this for more than seven days with no issues.
  • Medjai sticker – This is a symbol of your sacred duty.
  • Combat mask – Wearing this may help you unlock memories of your past life.

event.title: <show-title>Project Hail Mary</show-title> <event-type-title>Book Club Screening</event-type-title>
event.headline: Free sticker and bookmark for book readers
event.description:
Andy Weir fans, rejoice! At this special screening for book readers, you’ll get a free bookmark passport with a custom PROJECT HAIL MARY sticker to track your novel-to-movie journey. Whether you got your first sticker at WUTHERING HEIGHTS or are making your maiden Book Club voyage, join us and find out if the Rocky in your head matches the one depicted on screen.

Select locations will also feature a Book Fair in the bar after the movie, with local booksellers offering their wares while you sip a draft beer or cocktail:

  • Cedars
  • DTLA
  • Littleton
  • Lower Manhattan
  • Raleigh
  • South Lamar

eventType.title: Book Club Screening
eventType.headline: Collect custom stickers as you enjoy your favorite movie adaptations
eventType.description: Celebrate your favorite book-turned-movie with the Alamo Drafthouse Movie Book Club. Get your passport at the show, then fill it with custom-themed stickers at each Book Club screening. Join us in the bar after the film to connect with local bookstores, libraries, and other book lovers. Keep reading and keep watching!

event.title: <show-title>The Super Mario Galaxy Movie</show-title> <event-type-title>Family Party</event-type-title>
event.headline: Bring the little toadstools to our interactive Mario party
event.description: Warp into a whole new level with THE SUPER MARIO GALAXY MOVIE Family Party, an exclusive movie screening with a live host and interactive props. Gear up with squishy stars, glow-in-the-dark straws, and star wands worthy of Rosalina herself. There’s even a die-cut mask menu with a coloring page and crayons to take home. Show up early for our pre-show challenge and you could score the ultimate loot drop: a Yoshi Popcorn Bucket.

show.title: Britannia Hospital
show.headline: The chaotic workings of a hospital with staff on strike

event.title: <show-title>Britannia Hospital</show-title> <event-type-title>Free Victory Screening</event-type-title>
event.headline: Programmed by Gorillaz, ahead of their album The Mountain
event.description:
To celebrate the release of The Mountain on Feb. 27, the band Gorillaz is taking over Alamo Drafthouse with a full month of Guest Selects screenings, each programmed by a different member of the world’s most animated band.

On March 19, Noodle caps things off with BRITANNIA HOSPITAL, Lindsay Anderson’s gloriously unhinged 1982 dystopian satire set inside a crumbling NHS hospital and featuring, among other things, a super-brain in a jar. The film will be preceded by a short film, Gorillaz Presents The Mountain, The Moon Cave and The Sad God.

show.title: André is an Idiot
show.headline: A humorous and existential journey of André, a man with terminal colon cancer

event.title: <show-title>André is an Idiot</show-title><event-type-title> with Live Q&A</event-type-title>
event.headline: Director     Tony Benna Live in Person
event.description:
In-person Q&A with director Tony Benna following the screening

Unexpected, irreverent, and defiantly heartfelt, ANDRÉ IS AN IDIOT is a wildly life-affirming film about death and the strange clarity that arrives when time suddenly becomes finite. When André Ricciardi, a self-proclaimed “idiot” for skipping the colonoscopy that could have saved him, learns he is dying, he turns his final chapter into an experiment in radical honesty, humor, and curiosity. A lifelong iconoclast and ad-industry provocateur, André has never done anything the conventional way, and confronting mortality proves no different: he picks up a camera, cracks a joke, and begins to ask the biggest questions of his life with disarming candor.

In a deeply collaborative partnership with director Tony Benna, André’s inner world – his stories, fears, philosophies, and absurdist imagination – is brought to life through fantastical stop-motion animation woven into vérité footage. These animated interludes illuminate the way André processes both joy and dread, transforming the film into a playful yet profound portrait of a man determined to face death as openly and ridiculously as he has lived.

ANDRÉ IS AN IDIOT is ultimately a celebration of resilience, creative audacity, and the kind of fearless humor that refuses to let darkness have the last word. It invites audiences to rethink what “normal” looks like in the face of sickness and loss – and to consider that sometimes, the best way to survive life’s cruelties is simply to laugh at them.

show.title: Mermaid (2026)
show.headline: Florida man saves injured mermaid and nurses her back to health

event.title: <show-title>Mermaid (2026)</show-title><event-type-title> with Live Q&A</event-type-title>
event.headline: Actor     Johnny Pemberton Live in Person
event.description:
In-person Q&A with actor/executive producer Johnny Pemberton following the screening.

Doug is an awkward Florida drug addict. Divorced and alone, he shares custody of his young daughter with his ex. Doug's reeling from losing his job at a local strip club, where he takes care of the fish in the aquarium. Things are beyond bleak, so Doug zips his boat out into the Gulf to end the pain of his reality. But at his lowest point, he discovers an injured mermaid creature clinging to life. For Doug, it's a sign from the Universe, so he brings her home, gives her shelter in his tub, and begins to nurse her back to health. He even names her Destiny. As their rapport blossoms, word of his secret gets out and he is forced to protect his new friend by any means necessary.

show.title: Touch Me
show.headline: Two codependent best friends become addicted to having sex with an alien

event.title: <show-title>Touch Me</show-title><event-type-title> with Live Q&A</event-type-title>
event.headline: Director     Addison Heimann Live in Person
event.description:
In-person Q&A following the screening with filmmaker Addison Heimann.

After a series of unfortunate events leaves their house uninhabitable and reeking of poo, two co-dependent friends, Joey and Craig, find themselves homeless and out of options. That's when Joey's mysterious ex resurfaces. He wants her back. Along with being hot, Brian also happens to be an alien whose touch makes anxiety and depression disappear. The two venture to his compound for the weekend with the hope of being healed from past traumas, but underneath Brian’s veneer of healing is a sinister plot filled with murder, mayhem, and blood. TOUCH ME is a psychosexual horror-comedy about the nature of co-dependent friendships and how far we are willing to go for a slice of happiness.

show.title: The Serpent's Skin
show.headline: I see no evil. I have no body. My spirit's covered. She's always in me.

event.title: <show-title>The Serpent's Skin</show-title><event-type-title> with Live Q&A</event-type-title>
event.headline: With director Alice Maio Mackay in attendance.
event.description:
Twentysomething Anna (Alexandra McVicker, OCTOBER CROW) finally leaves her small, transphobic hometown for good and quickly begins dating goth tattoo artist Gen (Avalon Fast, CASTRATION MOVIE) after the two discover they share supernatural powers. But before Anna can call up the U-Haul, Gen inadvertently summons a demon through the sick neck tattoo she gives sensitive guitarist Danny (Jordan Dulieu). As Danny — or whatever’s inside of him — begins feeding off their friends, it’s up to Anna and Gen to figure out how to use their powers to save everyone before it’s too late.

The sixth-and-latest feature by 20-year-old filmmaking force of nature Alice Maio Mackay (CARNAGE FOR CHRISTMAS, Frameline48), THE SERPENT'S SKIN like a trans take on THE CRAFT or the YA horror paperback you wished you had in high school. Returning to the same premise as her early short THE SERPENT'S NEST (2021) and working with a group of trans film luminaries — including producer Louise Weard (CASTRATION MOVIE, Frameline49), editor Vera Drew (THE PEOPLE'S JOKER), and colorist Jessica Dunn Rovinelli (SO PRETTY), all remarkable filmmakers in their own right — THE SERPENT'S SKIN sees the full blossoming of the bold neo-Araki aesthetic that has made Mackay one of the most exciting voices in this current wave of independent trans cinema. (Frameline)

show.title: Céline and Julie Go Boating
show.headline: Alice in Wonderland meets French New Wave

event.title: <show-title>Céline and Julie Go Boating</show-title>
event.headline: Jacques Rivette’s magical mystery tour of female friendship and whimsy
event.description:
“I always let myself be overcome by irrepressible waves of feeling.”

Does it ever feel like you and your closest friend live in your own little universe? Inside jokes no one else would understand, secret languages born of meaningless moments, the strange ability to communicate telepathically with a simple glance. Some relationships, romantic or platonic, have a mystical air, a touch of destiny. In Jacques Rivette's CÉLENE AND JULIE GO BOATING, magic brings two young women, Célene (Juliet Berto), a cabaret magician, and Julie (Dominique Labourier), a librarian, together and leads them down the rabbit hole, if you will, into a mysterious world of absurdity and *GASP* murder. To quote critic Beatrice Loayza, the pair engage in “play as a life force.” Célene and Julie take turns swapping identities, move in together (U-Hauling lol), and traverse time and space through a “house of fiction” with the help of magical candy…. There’s a lot going on here, and it’s all so very French!

A celebration of female friendship and “pleasure as a form of liberation,” CÉLENE AND JULIE GO BOATING is a remarkable film filled to the brim with imagination and disruption. Think BEING JOHN MALKOVICH with less existential dread, or MULHOLLAND DRIVE with less existential dread. Inspired by Alice in Wonderland, Věra Chytilová’s DAISIES, and the works of Marcel Proust and Jean Cocteau, CÉLENE AND JULIE GO BOATING brings whimsy and joyful femininity to the French New Wave, and it is a must-see for anyone who has ever wanted to follow an impulse beyond the threshold of expectation. It doesn’t hurt to fall off the moon.

show.title: Romancing in Thin Air
show.headline: Rarified love in Shangri-La

event.title: <show-title>Romancing in Thin Air</show-title>
event.headline: Rarified love in Shangri-La
event.description:
After being dumped publicly at the altar by his co-star, famous actor Michael (Louis Koo) falls into a drunken stupor that leads him to hide away in a truck that whisks him up into the snowy mountains of Shangri-La. The truck in question belongs to Sue (Sammi Cheng Sau-Man), a widow in denial that her late husband, who got lost in the woods nearby, is gone forever. As Michael starts healing himself in the tranquility of Sue’s quaint motel, he begins to fall in love with her.

ROMANCING IN THIN AIR director Johnnie To is most well known for his action and crime thrillers; his foray into melodramatic romance – a subgenre that is a staple in East Asian cinema – is touching, sweet, and deeply melancholic. Although To’s film definitely hits all the tropes that define romantic dramas, it’s also a love letter to storytelling and how cinema draws people to each other.

show.title: Scream 7
show.headline: Do you like scary sequels?

show.title: Evilspeak
show.headline: Remember the kittle kid you used to pick on? Well, he's a big boy now.

event.title: <show-title>Evilspeak</show-title>
event.headline: Remember the kittle kid you used to pick on? Well, he's a big boy now.
event.description:
It's a well-known fact that computers are Lucifer's Weapon of Mass Destruction in his continuing battle against the human race. But at the apex of mankind's evolution (aka 1981), ol' Beelzebub needed only a Commodore 64 and dot-matrix printer to unleash an apocalyptic storm of demonic possession and extermination. Legendary character actor Clint Howard (THE ICE CREAM MAN, THE WRAITH) stars in this harrowing, vicious tale of tortured military school dweeb Stanley. When pushed to the brink of human endurance by his bullying classmates and the drunken, rape-crazy elderly janitor ("I'm gonna show you how I turn little boys into little GIRLS!"), Stanley allies with an electronic incarnation of The Dark Lord to level the playing field. Featuring blazing decapitations, fountainous gore, man-eating Hell-hogs, and enough clergical dismemberment and digital anti-Christian imagery to forever reinforce the fact that Jesus Christ is a goddamn wimp. Hail Satan slash sideways smiley face dot wink. (Zack)

35mm courtesy of University of North Carolina School of the Arts

show.title: The Visitor
show.headline: The sci-fi/horror epic that 1979 couldn't handle.

event.title: <show-title>The Visitor</show-title>
event.headline: "The Mount Everest of insane '70s Italian movies."
event.description:
The sci-fi/horror epic that 1979 couldn't handle.

An intergalactic warrior joins a cosmic Christ figure in battle against a demonic eight-year-old girl and her pet hawk, while the fate of the universe hangs in the balance!

One of the holiest of all Holyfuckingshits, THE VISITOR is a wonderful mishmash of THE OMEN, THE BIRDS, ROSEMARY’S BABY, STAR WARS, and CLOSE ENCOUNTERS – but even that barely hints at the fervor with which director Giulio Paradisi hurls his cast (John Huston, Glenn Ford, Shelley Winters, Franco Nero, Lance Henriksen, and, yes, even Sam Peckinpah) into the cinematic void. Just when you think you've nailed down which direction the film’s heading in, THE VISITOR shatters your notion of the time-space continuum with a blackhearted blowout of interplanetary possession, telekinetic avian assault, exploding basketballs, and ecclesiastical laserstorms. If you miss out on this one, then you have as much regard for cinema as you do for a discarded toenail clipping.

event.title: <show-title>Twin Peaks: The Return</show-title> <event-type-title>Parts 10-12</event-type-title>
event.description:
Part 10 | 60 mins.
The search for Cooper continues; more pieces of the puzzle come together. Richard Horne's violent behavior escalates, and Dougie's family faces danger.

Part 11 | 60 mins.
Events in Twin Peaks take a dark turn; Dougie's fate hangs in the balance. The FBI moves closer to finding Cooper, and a child is hit by a car.

Part 12 | 60 mins.
The investigation leads to unexpected places; familiar faces return. Audrey Horne makes a troubling appearance, and Sarah Palmer's behavior is erratic.

marketStrings.value: The personal closed captioning device displays feature film dialogue and other audio in the form of English-language text for individuals who are deaf or hard of hearing.

The descriptive audio headset provides individuals who are blind or have low vision with an English-language narration of the details of the feature presentation that can't be understood by the soundtrack alone.

Upon request at the box office, we provide assistive listening headsets with amplified audio for those individuals who are hard of hearing. For guests who prefer to use their own hearing aids, neck loops, or cochlear implants, our assistive listening headsets have a 3.5mm headphone jack to connect to those devices.

In order to ensure the highest quality experience for all guests, you may not enter a movie after it has started. We’d love to see you up to 30 minutes early. Running late? No worries, we will happily exchange your ticket for a different showtime or a raincheck to a future show.

sessionAttributes.name: Baby Day Show
sessionAttributes.description: For our baby day shows, we keep the lights up a bit, and the sound down a bit, so your little one never has to know they even went to the movies!

sessionAttributes.name: Kid Friendly
sessionAttributes.description: Grab the fam and let the fun begin! Our Kid Friendly screenings include pre-show activity sheets and crayons, a relaxed noise policy, and kids menus. Ages 3+ welcome with a parent or guardian.
